channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.eurogamer.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.eurogamer.net

item.title: Carcass Clad, the Mouthwashing team's next game, is a horrifying co-op tank game, announced at the PC Gaming Show
item.content:encoded:
The developers of Mouthwashing - the sci-fi horror hit - have announced their next game: Carcass Clad.

Announced at the PC Gaming Show, Wrong Organ's game is available to wishlist on Steam right now, with no release date established as of writing. You can watch the reveal trailer for it below.

In the trailer, we can see plenty of claustrophobic tank operations happening in a dark and haunting ruined cityscape. The tank is, as the title implies, clad in corpses. Many of them are still moving and bleating, hauntingly. Looks horrid! Can't wait.

The game's Steam page comes with the following description, which acts as a good tone setter for what to expect: "Featuring weighty physical controls, crew the Yksiö with a team of 3. Fuel is limited and ammo is scarce. The city's saint, long since laid to rest, walks again - gilded and defiled."

Mouthwashing was an excellent debut release from Wrong Organ, unflinching and refreshing. It took home multiple awards following its release, and sold exceptionally well across PC and console. The game also became something of a social media darling, winning over a community of passionate fans who I'm sure are looking forward to whatever horrors Carcass Clad has in store for them.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/carcass-clad-reveal-mouthwashing-next-game
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Cassette Beasts 2002 is the sequel to 2023's monster-collecting RPG, and it's coming to PC and consoles with multiplayer from the get-go
item.content:encoded:
Many indie studios have tried to replicate Pokémon's formula, but few have actually cracked the code. In 2023, Bytten Studio got it right with Cassette Beasts, mainly because it had something to add to the mix and wasn't entirely built on nostalgia. Now, the developers aim to take it to the next level with Cassette Beasts 2002.

Yes, the just-announced sequel will take us to the early 21st century in London, although its first trailer - which you can watch below - teases an adventure across two different worlds and more than a few twists.

There's a healthy dose of Digimon in there too, of course, but I'm inclined to say Bytten has a firm grasp on how to deliver something that speaks for itself. Moreover, it's been confirmed this RPG will launch with cross-platform multiplayer from the get-go via its Steam page. As confirmed by the trailer, it's coming to PC, PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and Nintendo Switch 2.

"After arriving in a quiet neighbourhood of London, you soon find yourself pulled down a rabbit hole to Nodnol, a land of beasts and high strangeness that exists in the shadow of our own," the official description reads.

Once again, Cassette Beasts' impressive fusion system is at the centre of the experience, with "over 57,000+ powerful and fully-animated fusion forms" available in the sequel. 12 companions can also be met, recruited, and even romanced, with "their own motivations, stories, and gameplay perks" stacking on top of the monster-catching and turn-based combat. Publisher Raw Fury hasn't revealed a release window yet, but more details are coming "over the next few months".

Mentioned in this article

Cassette Beasts

Android, Nintendo Switch, PC, Xbox One, Xbox Series X/S, iOS



https://www.eurogamer.net/cassette-beasts-2002-announcement
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Xbox seeks to clarify its approach to console exclusive games, after last night's PS5 snub for Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution caught man
item.content:encoded:
Xbox has shed more light on the company's exclusivity strategy, after last night's showcase threw a few surprises our way.

During the showcase, we got word of two Xbox console exclusives: Clockwork Revolution and Gears of War: E-Day. Up until last night, the assumption had been that Gears of War: E-Day would also be released on PlayStation 5. A PEGI rating still had it listed as a PS5 release just moments before Gears of War: E-Day's trailer aired, and allegedly this late change in platforms also caught some retailers anticipating a PlayStation release off guard.

But, while Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution are both Xbox console exclusives (and not times ones, at that), other games coming from the company's first party studios are not. The likes of Fable and Halo: Campaign Evolved have both been developed for PlayStation 5 as well, leaving some to question what exactly Xbox's strategy is. Following the successful launches of Sea of Thieves, Indiana Jones and the Great Circle, and more on PS5 and Nintendo Switch, is Xbox pulling back on multi-platform releases, or is it not?

Following last night's reveals, Xbox's Chief Content Officer Matt Booty spoke with Gamertag Radio about just that. He said the company wanted to give players a reason to purchase an Xbox and become an Xbox fan, while also rewarding those who have supported the company for many years now. "We know that exclusives are important," Booty said, highlighting Gears of War: E-Day and Clockwork Revolution's Xbox releases.

That being said, Booty added that Xbox's big multiplayer and live service games will continue to be multiplatform. "If we've promised something to players already, we are going to honour that promise," Booty stated, adding exclusivity will be determined on a case-by-case basis.

The exec closed by saying that when Xbox announces the release date of a game, it will also announce its launch platforms. "We're going to make the right decision, not the fast decision," Booty stressed, echoing similar words made by Xbox's newly-appointed CEO Asha Sharma.

Since her appointment in February of this year, Sharma has been steadily leaving her mark on the Xbox (or XBOX) brand. In May, she revealed the company would be sunsetting its Copilot initiative, which was once envisioned as an AI-driven "personal gaming sidekick", designed to offer real-time advice in games such as Minecraft. Sharma has also made changes to Xbox's Game Pass model, scrapped the "This is an Xbox" campaign, and started pointing attention to the next generation of Xbox hardware, so far known only as Project Helix. This hardware will "lead in performance", Sharma said, though when exactly we will see it remains something of a mystery.

As Sharma continues work on fixing the Xbox brand image, there is a growing call from the BDS movement to boycott the gaming platform due to parent company Microsoft's reported complicity in Israeli war crimes against the Palestinian population and state.

For everything else announced last night, including Resonance: A Plague Legacy, Metro 2039 and more, be sure to check out Lottie's roundup: Everything announced in Microsoft's Xbox Games Showcase.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/xbox-console-exclusive-strategy-clockwork-revolution-gears-of-war-e-day
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie crosses $1bn worldwide, but its predecessor still retains the video game adaptation crown
item.content:encoded:
Despite making the jump to video-on-demand on digital platforms last month, The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is still holding its theatrical position in cinemas in some locations. Now, it's finally pushed its global box office haul past the $1bn mark.

Deadline shared the update on 7th June, a little over two months after its original debut. It's an impressive feat that makes it 2026's first film to cross the big milestone, and while it's likely its theatrical run will extend for a while still, 2023's The Super Mario Bros. Movie currently has the lead with $1.36bn. Nonetheless, this means Nintendo, Universal Pictures, and Illumination have the two highest-grossing big-screen video game adaptations of all time now.

Domestically, The Super Mario Galaxy Movie also has the crown, with $428.5m making it the biggest film of 2026 in the United States so far. The overseas haul has reached $571.5m, which makes it the top Motion Picture Association title. Chinese sports comedy film Pegasus 3 reigns supreme in the international box office with $655m, made almost entirely in China alone.

Though critics weren't very kind to The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, it's clearly landed a critical hit on its target audience, which half-explains why Shigeru Miyamoto has been fiercely batting for Illumination's work on the sequel. A third instalment seems guaranteed, but Illumination's next Nintendo flick - set to be released in 2028 - could be Donkey Kong starring Seth Rogen.

Eurogamer's two-star review of The Super Mario Galaxy Movie pointed out that, despite some dazzling visuals and bursts of imagination, there's "little else beneath the spectacle". It gave us the glorious return of Star Fox before the new game was announced, though.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/the-super-mario-galaxy-movie-one-billion-box-office
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fable is getting some post-launch DLC already, with an extended look at gameplay planned for later this week
item.content:encoded:
Playground Games has announced some post-launch DLC for Fable, months out from its release date in February of next year.

Fable: Order of the Hero will add a new region to the game, this "story-driven expansion" has you discover the mystery behind a cult hidden in Albion.

Instead of wiping them out, you'll be able to lead the cult in your chosen direction. Be it heroic, naughty, or something else, you'll become their overseer and determine the future of this strange group.

Being announced so far before launch, there's no word on when exactly this will be released, nor the price of this DLC. In fact, the Fable trailer released at the Xbox Games Showcase last night didn't appear to cover it, so it's peculiar timing.

In addition, there will be an extended look at Fable gameplay released to the public this Wednesday, 10th June. So this seems to have turned into a big week for Fable fans, all things considered.

Mentioned in this article



https://www.eurogamer.net/fable-post-launch-dlc-gameplay
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: After over five years in early access, Valheim is finally graduating to version 1.0
item.content:encoded:
Viking survival and crafting hit, Valheim, which first appeared in early access in early 2021, is almost ready for its full release. Developer Iron Gate announced at the PC Gaming Show that the game will officially launch on 9th September.

The release arrives alongside the last major update for the game, which we've known about for a while, too. Titled The Deep North, the update includes a new frozen biome, new enemies, weapons, bases, and more besides.

On that date, the game's PC, Xbox One, Xbox Series X/S, Linux, and Mac versions will be updated to version 1.0. Iron Gate also confirmed that that's when the PlayStation 5 version, announced last year, will be released. The Nintendo Switch 2 version will likewise arrive on the same date.

We've been expecting the 1.0 for a little while now, so it's good to see all of the details nice and official. The even better news is that cross-play will be supported across all platforms, so everyone can play together from day one. Of course, you'll have to start a new world so everyone can experience the new content from the beginning.

When it first debuted in February 2021, Valheim was an immediate success. It sold 3m units in 16 days, and peaked at nearly 500,000 concurrent players on Steam, making it one of the platform's biggest releases that year.

Mentioned in this article

Valheim

Mac, Nintendo Switch 2, PC, PS5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X/S



https://www.eurogamer.net/valheim-version-1-0-release-date
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
